title: Study on the Effect of Co-N-C Doping on the Catalytic Performance of RuO2/MnO2 Catalysts in Li-O2 Batteries
authors: Qunying Kang, Hong Sun, Tianyu Zhang, Mingfu Yu and Xilin Guo
abstract: Li-O2 batteries have attracted widespread attention due to their high theoretical energy density, but their practical application is limited by the slow kinetics and high overpotentials of the ORR and OER. Developing efficient electrocatalysts is key to overcoming this bottleneck. In this study, we successfully synthesized a Co, N, and C-doped RuO2/MnO2 composite catalyst via ultrasound-assisted hydrothermal synthesis and vacuum filtration, and subsequently formed a Co-N-C-RuO2/MnO2 composite cathode on a carbon paper substrate. Through systematic material characterization, electrochemical tes…

title: Interconnected NaVO3 Nanostructures Grown on Carbon Nanotube Networks for High-Performance <mark>Sodium-Ion</mark> Batteries
authors: Chunmei Wang, Tingting Fan, Lei Cheng, Ying-Peng Xie, Shi Qiu and Songtao Li
abstract: Sodium vanadate (NaVO3) has attracted increasing attention as an anode material for <mark>sodium-ion</mark> batteries owing to its high theoretical capacity. However, its practical application is severely hindered by poor electronic conductivity and pronounced structural instability during cycling. Here we report an interconnected NaVO3–carbon nanotube (CNT) hybrid architecture in which NaVO3 nanostructures are in situ grown along a multi-walled CNT framework via an evaporation-induced solution process. This rationally designed composite establishes continuous electron-transport pathways while mechanically…

title: Quantifying Energy Storage Density Utilization Trends in Redox Flow Battery Chemistries
authors: Ridge M. Bachman, Isaac K. Gamwo and Derek M. Hall
abstract: The transition to new energy resources is driving the need for efficient energy storage. Redox flow batteries (RFBs) are a promising solution due to their flexible design and safer materials, particularly for long-duration storage applications. Despite these advantages, RFB technologies still have considerable room for growth in terms of utilizing all of their available energy storage density. Herein, we assess RFB energy storage density utilization trends for multiple chemistries by accounting for their unique thermodynamic limits, energy efficiencies, and charge capacity constraints. title: Feasibility Validation and Impact Analysis of Mass Fraction for PDMS-Based Fluorine-Free Microporous Layers
authors: Yu Zhu, Yanpeng Dong, Zhiguang Qian and Shixue Wang
abstract: Commercial fluorinated microporous layers (MPLs) for proton exchange membrane fuel cells (PEMFCs) face regulatory challenges due to perfluorooctanoic acid (PFOA) emissions. This study proposes a fluorine-free hydrophobic strategy using polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS), prepared via a binary solvent method to replace polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE). Electrochemical performance and microstructure are systematically benchmarked against commercial 20 wt% PTFE-MPL. Results indicate that PDMS forms a uniform, isotropic interconnected porous network, avoiding the directional cracks observed in PTFE-MPLs. A…

title: Stabilization of Zero-Excess <mark>Sodium</mark> Metal Anodes Using LiPF6
authors: Louis V. Morris, James L. Hart, Zoey Warecki, Corey Love, Rachel E. Carter and Gordon H. Waller
abstract: The adoption of <mark>sodium</mark> metal anodes is limited due to their poor <mark>coulombic efficiency</mark> stemming from dendritic plating morphologies and incomplete <mark>solid electrolyte</mark> interface (<mark>SEI</mark>) formation. This study presents a <mark>sodium</mark> metal battery electrolyte formulation utilizing <mark>lithium</mark> hexafluorophosphate as an additive, which selectively decomposes at the <mark>sodium</mark> metal interface to form a highly uniform, compact, and inorganic-rich <mark>SEI</mark> layer. This improved passivation layer enables >99% columbic efficiency operation of zero-excess <mark>sodium</mark> metal anodes. The mechanism of action of this additive is further e…

title: Ex Situ Pre-Lithiation of Silicon-Based Anode Materials
authors: Shiba P. Adhikari, Nicholas P. W. Pieczonka, Sayed Youssef Sayed, Jeffrey D. Cain and Jianlin Li
abstract: The implementation of silicon (Si) and silicon oxide (SiOx) anodes for lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) is hindered by first-cycle capacity loss and low <mark>Coulombic efficiency</mark> due to unstable <mark>solid electrolyte</mark> <mark>interphase</mark> (SEI) formation, which consumes <mark>lithium</mark> and leads to poor cycle life. Here, we present an electrochemical continuous stirred tank reactor (ECSTR) as a platform for ex situ pre-lithiation of Si and SiOx anode materials. Unlike conventional techniques, which suffer from poor <mark>lithium</mark> distribution and limited scalability, our ECSTR enables uniform, controlled lithiation of Si-based powd…

abstract: Published online: 05 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-74049-1 Sulfide-based all-solid-state <mark>lithium</mark> batteries severely suffer from <mark>sulfide electrolyte</mark>/Li interfacial instability. abstract: Published online: 03 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02604-8 Polyelectrolyte elastomers support selective and leakage-free ionic transport, but suffer from low <mark>ionic conductivity</mark>. A solid-state dielectric additive is introduced to various polyelectrolyte elastomers, which increases <mark>ionic conductivity</mark> by two orders of magnitude and maintains elasticity and leakage-free properties.

authors: Rachel E. Clarke
James M. Otis
abstract: Published online: 04 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-73905-4 Avoiding danger even when competing goals are imminent supports survival, yet the mechanisms orchestrating this behavioral override remain unclear. Macdonald and colleagues1 describe a thalamostriatal microcircuit that modulates terminal dopamine release, bypassing canonical midbrain dopamine circuitry to prioritize <mark>safety</mark>.

title: Sensor Driven Long Short-Term Memory Gate Recurrent Estimation with Deep Reinforcement Learning Based Charge Control for Battery Swapping Station Application
authors: Arun Chendhuran R, Senthil Kumar J and Sivaraju S. S.
abstract: Electric vehicle battery swapping technology can promote the high-quality development of new energy vehicles. Rapid analysis of the state of charge (SOC) is particularly essential for electric vehicle battery swapping. Traditional SOC assessment methods vary in model types and simplification techniques, often leading to skewed SOC estimation and unfair battery-swapping transactions in electric vehicles. To overcome this issue, this paper proposes an integrated method that accurately estimates SOC and controls charging current according to environmental conditions using an LSTM-based Gated Recu…

title: Electrochemical Performance, Degradation Mechanisms, and Thermal Effects in Starting Batteries: A Comprehensive Review
authors: Talal F. Qahtan, Hussein K. Amusa, Idris K. Popoola and Satam Alotibi
abstract: Starting batteries are critical electrochemical energy storage systems that deliver high power pulses for engine ignition and auxiliary electrical functions. Their performance, durability, and safety are governed by complex electrochemical interactions involving electrode materials, electrolytes, interfacial processes, and operating conditions. This review provides a comprehensive electrochemical perspective on major starting battery technologies, including flooded lead–acid, absorbed glass mat, gel cell, lithium-ion, and nickel–cadmium batteries. Key electrochemical performance-limiting facto…

title: Interpretable Battery SOH Prediction: A Comparative Interpretability Framework for Multi-Architecture ML Models
authors: Shafiyee Islam and Gon Namkoong
abstract: This work introduces a unified interpretability-efficiency framework for lithium-ion battery state of health (SOH) prediction using hybrid deep learning architectures. We comparatively analyze four hybrid models: CNN LSTM MultiHead, CNN Feature Extractor LSTM, DNN LSTM, and DNN BiLSTM to disentangle how network topology, feature composition, and computational design influence both predictive fidelity and physical interpretability. By integrating Monte Carlo Shapley (MC Shapley), background occlusion SHAP (BoSHAP), and ablation analysis, we quantify the contribution and robustness of five elect…

title: Autonomous Classification and Prediction of Degradation Transitions in Lithium-Ion Batteries
authors: Yixin Nie and Weiyu Li
abstract: Lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) often exhibit a two-stage degradation behavior, transitioning from gradual capacity fade to accelerated aging at the degradation knee point. Accurate and timely assessment of a battery’s degradation stage relative to this knee is critical for battery management. However, most existing degradation-stage classification and knee-proximity prediction approaches rely on multi-cycle historical data, high-dimensional time-series inputs, or complex deep learning architectures, and are typically evaluated under a single knee definition, limiting interpretability and practic…

authors: Jiaao Li, Ao Li, Weikang Li, Shanhai Zhao, Shanlin Tong and Yongsheng Liu
abstract: Accurate and reliable joint estimation of state of charge (SOC) and state of health (SOH) is a core requirement for next-generation battery management systems (BMSs). In this context, this study develops an innovative joint estimation algorithm that combines a fractional-order sliding-mode adaptive multi-innovation unscented Kalman filter with an extended Kalman filter (FOSMOAMIUKF+EKF). The proposed method is based on a fractional-order model, and the Crested Porcupine Optimizer (CPO) is introduced for the first time to identify the model parameters accurately. abstract: High-throughput <mark>electrochemical impedance</mark> spectroscopy (EIS) offers significant potential for the rapid characterization of battery electrode materials and the extraction of descriptors for data-driven materials discovery. In this work, we present over 700 EIS spectra measured in a semi-automated workflow of up to 64 electrode samples in a combinatorial electrochemical cell with liquid electrolyte in half-cells, symmetric cells and anodeless cells. abstract: Polymer-based solid-state electrolytes are promising for next-generation <mark>lithium</mark> metal batteries, yet their limited <mark>ionic conductivity</mark> and mechanical stability at ambient conditions remain key challenges. Herein, we report a novel gradient crosslinked polymer electrolyte (PU/PUA/PU) synthesized via a sequential in situ UV-curing process that integrates a mechanically robust polyurethane acrylate (PUA) core with soft linear polyurethane (PU) interfaces. This all-solid membrane operates without any liquid plasticizer and the interfacial PU layers ensure low interfacial resistance and intimate el…

title: Modified Archimedes Method for the Measurement of Average Porosity of Lithium-Ion Battery Electrodes and Its Comparison with Standard Techniques
authors: Rabin Siva Dev, Gerrit Karl Mertin, Marc Oldenburger, Dominik Wycisk, Jonas Günther, Ralf Moos and Jan Philipp Schmidt
abstract: This work presents a simple method for obtaining highly reproducible and precise values of the average porosity of electrodes used in lithium-ion batteries. The method was developed by modifying the classical Archimedes method (hence referred to as the Modified Archimedes Method or MAM) and employs dimethyl carbonate (DMC) as the immersion liquid to closely mimic electrolyte infiltration. All aspects of the method, measurement setup design, experimental protocol, error propagation, and validation of results, were systematically assessed. The wetting properties of DMC and battery electrolytes w…

title: BattScape, a Drag-and-Drop Electrochemical Battery Simulation Framework Implemented in MATLAB Simscape
authors: Rafał Pakuła, Williams Agyei Appiah, Mukul Parmananda, Bharatkumar Suthar and Johannes Landesfeind
abstract: Despite many commercial and academic approaches to simulate and predict battery operation, the complexity of software tools often limits their broad application. This work presents a novel physical domain library, called BattScape, that has been developed in Simscape from MathWorks® aiming at bridging the gap between experimentalists and simulation experts by providing a framework for user-friendly battery simulations. Our validation with an isothermal P2D Newman model with concentration dependent parameters in COMSOL Multiphysics® shows very good agreement for charge and discharge simulations…

title: Chemometric Models and Electrochemical Techniques for Studying the Health of Li-Ion Batteries: A Review
authors: E. Sandrucci, S. Brutti and F. Marini
abstract: This study presents a thorough analysis of machine learning techniques utilized for monitoring the health and predicting the remaining useful life (RUL) of lithium-ion batteries, which is essential for optimizing the performance of electric vehicles (EVs). Given the challenges faced by lithium-ion batteries, such as <mark>capacity fading</mark> and environmental factors, key indicators like State of Health (SOH) and RUL become vital. The paper reviews several adaptive machine learning methodologies, including Support Vector Regression (SVR), Gaussian Process Regression (GPR), and hybrid neural networks tha…

title: Revealing the Limitations of Lithium-Ion Batteries in Thermal Regeneration Electrochemical Cycle
authors: Yuansheng Xu, Yining Lao, Qingyun Dou, Shan Xu and Yuying Zheng
abstract: Harvesting low-grade environmental heat for electricity generation represents a sustainable strategy, but existing technologies struggle with efficiency and stability limitations. Although lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) are mature for energy storage, their capability for direct thermal-to-electrical energy conversion remains largely unexplored. In this study, we investigate commercial LIBs within a thermal regeneration electrochemical cycle (TREC). Contrary to expectations for thermal energy harvesting, our results consistently demonstrate no net energy output, because the thermally induced volt…

authors: Regina V. Khasanova, Elena V. Karaseva and Vladimir S. Kolosnitsyn
abstract: Long-term cathodic-anodic cycling (more than 570 cycles) of symmetrical lithium-lithium cells with a 1 M solution of LiN(SO2F)2 in sulfolane was studied at a current density of 0.2 mA cm−2, with a metallic lithium plating/stripping capacity of 1 mAh cm−2 and a temperature of 30 °C. An SEM study of the morphology and structure of lithium electrodes revealed that during cathodic-anodic cycling, a 100 μm-thick foil lithium metal electrode transforms into a three-layer structure with an overall thickness of about 250 μm. abstract: Published online: 14 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-73012-4 All-solid-state batteries have attracted widespread attention due to their potential for high specific energy and safety, but they struggle to operate stably under low pressure. abstract: This narrative summarizes, in retrospect, the enormous and quietly-conducted contribution of Dr Johan Coetzer, a South African scientist and entrepreneur, to the discovery, development and implementation of the high temperature sodium—metal chloride “ZEBRA” battery (Na/β-Al2O3, NaAlCl4/MCl2 (M = Ni, Fe)). title: Electrolyte Parameter Identification by High-Frequency Impedance Measurement During Current Pulses
authors: Conrad Bieneck, Lars von Kolzenberg, Carsten G. Staacke, Felix Riewald, Arnulf Latz and Timo Danner
abstract: Accurate electrolyte transport parameters are essential for reliable simulations of lithium-ion battery performance. This study presents a novel method to determine the diffusion coefficient and cationic transference number of liquid electrolytes by measuring high-frequency impedance spectra during pulse polarization. The pulse induces concentration polarization within the separator, which is detectable in changes in the high-frequency resistance. A pseudo-2D electrochemical model is fitted to the temporal evolution of the high-frequency resistance to extract the diffusion coefficient and tran…

title: A Bilayer Cathode Design Procedure for Li Ion Batteries Using the Multilayer Doyle-Fuller-Newman Model (M-DFN)
authors: E. C. Tredenick, A. M. Boyce, R. Drummond and S. R. Duncan
abstract: Heterogeneities in lithium ion batteries can be significant factors in electrode under utilisation and degradation while charging. Bilayer electrodes have been proposed as a convenient and scalable way to homogenise the electrode response. In this paper, the design of a bilayer cathode for Li-ion batteries composed of separate layers of lithium nickel manganese cobalt oxide Li[Ni0.6Mn0.2Co0.2]O2 (NMC622) and lithium iron phosphate LiFePO4 (LFP) is optimised using the multilayer Doyle-Fuller-Newman (M-DFN) model. Changes to the carbon binder domain, electrolyte volume fraction, and tortuosity p…

title: Interfacial Mechanics Regulation in Electrochemical Impedance Analysis of Anode-Free Solid-State Lithium Metal Batteries
authors: Zhiqiang Ling, Qian Zhang, Chuanwei Li, Siyuan Yang, Lipan Xin, Zhiyong Wang, Lin’an Li and Shibin Wang
abstract: Anode-free solid-state lithium metal batteries (AFSSLBs) form lithium metal anodes through in situ electrodeposition on the current collector surface during cycling. This design not only achieves higher energy density but also avoids the complex manufacturing processes associated with traditional lithium metal anodes. However, inhomogeneous lithium deposition during operation can lead to interfacial mechanical failure, severely degrading the battery’s electrochemical stability. This study investigates the correlation between interfacial mechanical properties and electrochemical performance in …

title: Galvanic Corrosion Behavior Between Copper (T2) and Carbon Steel (Q235) in Simulated Deep Geological Environment
authors: Ziang Su, Jie Pang, Yu Xin, Nazhen Liu, Xiangju Liu, Xiaoxuan Han, Weichen Xu and Xuwei Chen
abstract: In this study, the galvanic corrosion behavior of Cu and CS (carbon steel) in simulated Beishan groundwater was investigated. The influence of Cu:CS area ratio (up to 8000), temperature (20–70 °C), O2 content (groundwater sparged with air, N2, and in a N2-filled glove box), on galvanic current density (ig) was investigated. In the presence of sufficient O2, the slope of steady-state galvanic current density (ig∞) to Cu:CS area ratio indicates the segmented control process of galvanic corrosion, which are O2 reduction kinetic control, O2 diffusion control, and anodic reaction control. As Cu:CS …

title: Investigating a Localized High Concentration Electrolyte for Silicon-Containing Lithium-Ion Cells
authors: Jeffin James Abraham, Eytan Mendel-Elias, Saar Omessi, Chanmonirath (Michael) Chak, Lin Ma, Jessie Harlow, Michel B. Johnson, Michael Metzger and J. R. Dahn
abstract: Energy-dense lithium-ion (Li-ion) batteries are increasingly in demand for a wide range of energy storage applications. The introduction of silicon (Si) as a negative electrode candidate offers a promising route to meeting the high energy density requirements of next-generation Li-ion batteries. However, suitable electrolytes must be identified that can ensure both long cycle life and cell safety. In this study, a localized high-concentration electrolyte (LHCE) was evaluated for three different Si-containing negative electrode types: Chemical SiC (ChemSiC), micron-sized Si (μSi), and Mechanica…

title: Clustering and Prediction of Early Capacity Fade Trajectories in Lithium-Metal Batteries using Data-Driven Approaches
authors: Maitri Uppaluri, Le Xu, Wenting Ma, Nojan Aliahmad, Karl Littau and Simona Onori
abstract: Lithium-metal batteries (LMBs) are promising candidates for next-generation batteries due to their high energy density. However, during cycling, lithium forms dendritic structures on the negative electrode surface, leading to the formation of dead lithium and resulting in capacity loss. In this paper, we employ a dataset of lab-scale lithium-metal batteries cycled under various charging rates and fixed discharge rate. We apply machine learning models that leverage initial cycle data from these cells to predict the state-of-health (SOH) trajectories. The data reveals different forms of SOH traj…

title: Long-Term Durability Projection of PEMFC Electrodes Under Non-Accelerated Conditions
authors: Anusorn Kongkanand, Paul Yu, Venkata Yarlagadda, Wenbin Gu and Srikanth Arisetty
abstract: Mathematical modeling of performance and durability plays a central role in developing electrochemical devices, where complex trade-offs between functionality, longevity, and cost are frequently done. To shorten learning cycles when developing a proton exchange membrane fuel cell (PEMFC) to last tens of thousands of hours, accelerated stress tests (ASTs) are commonly used. However, this can mischaracterize degradation mechanisms leading to unpreferred outcomes. In this work, we investigate platinum (Pt) surface area loss and carbon support degradation under non-accelerated, realistic operating…

title: Performance Study of Cellulose Acetate/Aramid Fiber Separator for Lithium Batteries
authors: Wenlong Mu, Lianqin Wang, Shikun Zhang, Wencui Guo and Kaiyuan Zhou
abstract: To address the poor thermal stability and low electrolyte affinity of commercial polyolefin separators, as well as the low mechanical strength and weak flame retardancy of pure cellulose acetate (CA) separators, two sandwich-structured composite separators, namely cellulose acetate-aramid fiber-cellulose acetate (C-A-C) and aramid fiber-cellulose acetate-aramid fiber (A-C-A), were fabricated via layer-by-layer electrospinning. The design synergistically combines the high wettability of CA with the high strength, thermal resistance, and flame retardancy of aramid fiber (AF). The composite separ…

title: pH-Dependent Redox Kinetics and Mass-Transport Parameters of CoW12 Relevant to Redox Flow Batteries
authors: Jayant Dwivedi, Geetanksha Gupta, Himanshu Kumawat, Manoj Wary and Manoj Neergat
abstract: The redox reactions of K6[CoW12O40] (CoW12)—a polyoxometalate featuring two distinct redox centers (CoO4 (Co2+/Co3+) and W12O36 (W6+/W5+))—are investigated. Cyclic voltammetry (CV) and electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) are employed to study the effect of proton concentration in the pH range of 1–4 on the redox reaction kinetics and mass transport of the molecular ions in a three-electrode configuration. The charge-discharge capacity, power density, and cycling stability (reversibility) are found to be a strong function of pH. It is concluded that the pH is a critical parameter that …

title: Unravelling and Managing Thermal Behaviours of Lithium-Ion Batteries for eVTOLs via Optical Fiber Sensing
authors: Xuanhe Liang, Wu Meng, Xibin Lu and Jiaqiang Huang
abstract: Electric vertical take-off and landing aircraft (eVTOL) are poised to become a cornerstone of the low-altitude economy. However, the thermal behaviours of eVTOL batteries under mission-relevant operation remain poorly understood. Here, we implant optical-fiber sensors into commercial eVTOL cells to acquire operando internal temperature dynamics under an eVTOL duty protocol. By coupling these measurements with optical-fiber-based calorimetry, we reconstruct time-resolved heat-generation profiles with high accuracy and temporal resolution. Using the experimentally derived heat source terms, we f…

title: Resonant Flyback Converter-Based Cell-to-Cell SoC Equalization for Li-Ion Batteries in xEV Applications
authors: Sridivya Vattem, Srinivasarao Gorantla and Bharath Kumar N.
abstract: This paper describes a way to actively balance the cells in lithium-ion battery packs that are connected in series using a resonant flyback converter (RFBC). The proposed topology uses resonant energy transfer to achieve soft-switching operation and lower switching stress while keeping a single-converter architecture. Flyback-based balancing systems presented in the literature mostly use hard-switched operation or multiple converter stages. The energy flow from cells with a higher State of Charge (SoC) to a lower SoC cell is controlled by an event-driven SoC-based control algorithm which contr…

title: Urea-Derived Nitrogen as a Nitrogen Source for ZnMn2O4 Modification: Enhancing Zn2+ Transport Kinetics to Construct High-Performance Aqueous Zinc-Ion Batteries
authors: Cheng Xu, Zifan Wang, Gang Wang, Yong Lu, Qi Xu and Jingjing Qi
abstract: ZnMn2O4 (ZMO) spinel is regarded as a promising cathode material for aqueous zinc-ion batteries (AZIBs), owing to its outstanding Zn2+ storage capability. Its practical application in high-capacity cathodes is hindered by drawbacks, including low electrical conductivity, slow Zn2+ diffusion kinetics, and poor cycling stability. To address shortcomings, this study employs a nitrogen doping strategy (using three nitrogen sources) to partially substitute oxygen atoms in ZMO lattice with nitrogen atoms. Experimental and computational results demonstrate that nitrogen atoms replace oxygen atoms, fo…

title: Rigid-Flexible Coupling Strategy: 3D Polyimide Hosted Composite Electrolytes with LiDFOB for High Performance Lithium Metal Batteries
authors: Shixin Xiao, Meihua Liu and Guoqiang Li
abstract: Solid-state lithium metal batteries face critical challenges regarding ionic conductivity, mechanical strength, and interfacial instability. To address these issues, this study proposes a feasible solution: a composite solid electrolyte designed with a “rigid-flexible” coupling strategy. The electrolyte comprises a high-strength 3D porous polyimide nanofiber skeleton and a PVDF-HFP matrix filled with LATP ceramic fillers and modified by LiDFOB additives. As a result, the optimized composite exhibits remarkable performance, featuring a high tensile strength of 6.7 MPa and an excellent electroch…

title: Cathode Process in KCl-K2TiF6-TiO2 Molten Salt
authors: Evgeniy Koriakin
abstract: In this article, the cathode process in the KCl-K2TiF6-TiO2 melt at a temperature of 800 °C was studied using cyclic voltammetry. It was shown that the titanium electroreduction on a tungsten includes three electrode stages, as evidenced by the formation of peaks on the voltammograms at potentials of −0.25, −0.90, and −1.60 V relative to the tungsten electrode potential. Presumably, these stages correspond to the transitions Ti4+ → Ti3+, Ti3+ → Ti2+ and Ti2+ → Ti°, respectively. Based on the analysis of diagnostic dependencies, it is noted that all three electrode stages are limited by the dif…

title: Tuning interfacial polarity for stable high-potential lithium metal batteries
abstract: Published online: 13 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41565-026-02167-4 A molecular engineering strategy using dipolar self-assembled monolayers (SAMs) establishes interfacial polarity as a descriptor for battery electrode stability. By tuning the electronic structure of the SAM and, thus, nanometric surface environment of the positive electrode, stable operation of high-potential lithium metal batteries is demonstrated and validated through interface-sensitive in situ spectroscopy.

abstract: Lithium batteries are pivotal for modern energy storage, yet their performance and safety are critically influenced by electrolyte stability. This study systematically investigates three structurally similar fluorinated anhydrides, difluoroacetic anhydride (DFAA), trifluoroacetic anhydride (TFAA), and pentafluoropropionic anhydride (PFPA), as multifunctional electrolyte additives for lithium metal batteries. Electrochemical evaluations reveal that the addition of 1 wt% TFAA significantly enhances the cycling stability of both LiFePO4 (LFP) and LiNi0.6Co0.2Mn0.2O2 (NCM622) cathodes. After 400 c…

title: Ti-Oxide Doping Alleviated Defects and Introduced Ti–O–Si Bonds to Enhance the Cycling Stability of the Silicon‑Carbon Anode
authors: Songlin Yu, Pengtao Wang, Jiangtao Liang, Song Yang, Junhao Liu and Xuzhong Gong
abstract: Silicon-based anode materials have low electrical conductivity, volume expansion and the easy repeated formation of solid electrolyte interface layers, which seriously impede their commercialization. Introducing Ti–O–Si bonds into silicon-based materials has been proven to be an effective way to alleviate silicon defects. Here, we adopted a one-step mechanical force strategy to synthesize Ti–O–Si bonds, and high-energy ball milling was employed to promote the exfoliation of the lamellar structure of natural graphite spherical tailings and restructure it. During the process, nano-Si and Ti4O7 w…

title: Unlocking hidden sodium
authors: Yanan Sun
abstract: Published online: 12 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41560-026-02060-3 Fe-based polyanionic cathodes are promising for large-scale Na-ion batteries but are limited by incomplete Na utilization. Now, research shows that tuning the local Na coordination via V substitution in phosphate-based cathode allows additional Na sites to participate, enabling near-complete Na utilization, enhancing energy density and cycling stability.

abstract: Published online: 12 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41560-026-02059-w Fe-based polyanionic cathodes are promising for Na-ion batteries but are limited by inactive Na sites and irreversible Na loss. abstract: Formation is a critical manufacturing process for lithium-ion batteries, significantly influencing the formation of the solid electrolyte interphase (SEI) and the subsequent electrochemical and thermal performance of the cells. However, conventional constant-current formation often leads to non-uniform SEI growth and poor temperature consistency. In this work, a novel pulse formation strategy is proposed for LiFePO4/C lithium-ion batteries. title: From Aging Mechanism to Safety Response: A Framework for Lithium Plating Detection and Safety Assessment
authors: Philipp Moosmann, Katharina Bischof, Ilona Jipa, Markus Hölzle and Thomas Waldmann
abstract: Thermal runaway poses a substantial safety concern in high-energy lithium-ion batteries. Understanding aging-induced degradation and developing reliable detection strategies are, therefore, essential. This study compares the thermal safety characteristics of commercial 3.5 Ah 18650 cylindrical cells containing nickel-rich cathodes and graphite/SiOx anodes that had been aged using dynamic WLTP driving cycles and continuous laboratory cycling conditions with and without lithium plating at different SoH and SoC levels. This work focuses on the formation, detection, reversibility, and safety impli…

title: Capacity Gain in Li-Ion Cells with Silicon-Containing Electrodes
authors: Marco-Tulio F. Rodrigues, Charles McDaniel, Stephen E. Trask and Daniel P. Abraham
abstract: Silicon-containing lithium-ion batteries can exhibit capacity gain early in life, which makes forecasting future cell behavior difficult. We have observed these anomalous trends even in conditions where known mechanisms, such as overhang equalization and excessive electrolyte oxidation, are unlikely to be significant. Here, we combine simulations and experiments to analyze four cases that can produce increased capacity in Si cells. Three of these pathways relate to “break-in” processes, where improved mass and charge transport can lead to increased access to active electrode domains and decrea…

authors: Zheng Wang, Xiaolan Wu, Huijie Shi, Naixing Yang, Oluwatunmishe Sharon Banjoko and Zeyu Chen
abstract: During fast charging of lithium-ion batteries in electric vehicles, lithium plating severely degrades performance (e.g., capacity fade, impedance rise) and exacerbates safety risks (e.g., thermal runaway). To address this, we propose a voltage-driven adaptive charging strategy that dynamically regulates current profiles by leveraging the intrinsic correlation between terminal voltage and lithium-plating overpotential. This correlation is first quantified via an electrochemical-thermal-aging (ETA) coupling model. Our analysis reveals that polarization characteristics and plating susceptibility …

title: Non-Invasive Electrode Impedance Estimation for Optimized Charge Profile Parameterization of Lithium-Ion Batteries
authors: Cedric Kirst, Marco Düsdieker, Paul Gasper, Corey R. Randall, Ludwig Streller, Kandler Smith, Alexander Karger, Jan P. Singer and Andreas Jossen
abstract: This work presents a non-invasive method for parameterizing a physically motivated equivalent circuit model of lithium-ion batteries using operando electrochemical impedance spectroscopy and time-domain data. The proposed model consists exclusively of linear circuit elements, enabling computationally efficient simulation and real-time implementation on battery management system chips. By integrating frequency- and time-domain measurements, the model accurately estimates internal states such as the negative electrode potential, achieving a root mean square error of 12.3 mV during fast charging …

title: Direct Reuse of Degraded LiFePO4 Electrodes with LiBH4 as Chemical Lithiation Reagent
authors: Feifan Li, Chen Chen, Zhen Zheng and Lei Li
abstract: The recycling of spent lithium iron phosphate (S-LFP) batteries is crucial for sustainable energy development. Herein, we report a facile and efficient direct regeneration strategy for S-LFP electrodes via spontaneous chemical lithiation using LiBH4. By simply soaking the S-LFP electrodes in a 0.2 M LiBH4/THF solution at room temperature for 6 min, the Li+ loss is replenished and the crystal structure is effectively restored. The regenerated LFP (R-LFP) electrodes exhibit a high discharge capacity of 141.94 mAh g−1 at 0.5 C and retain over 71% capacity after 500 cycles. This method, operating …

authors: Gustavo Ramirez, Anna Halajko and Glenn G. Amatucci
abstract: Bismuth films deposited by electron beam deposition were explored as a potential negative electrode material in Li-ion batteries of high power, high volumetric energy density, and areal capacity in excess of 3 mAh cm−2. The effects of lithiation/delithiation rates on film cycle life are discussed along with nuances with respect to film thickness. The rate performance of the Bi films was evaluated, surpassing the critical combination of areal capacities and areal currents previously reported for Li alloy intermetallic films in literature. The observed areal current density, C rate capability, a…

title: Comparing the Gassing of Hard Carbon During Lithiation and Sodiation via a Novel Two-Compartment Online Electrochemical Mass Spectrometry Cell for Sodium-Ion Batteries
authors: Philip Rapp and Hubert A. Gasteiger
abstract: Interfacial degradation processes in lithium-ion and sodium-ion batteries frequently result in gas formation, making online electrochemical mass spectrometry (OEMS) a powerful tool to gain mechanistic insights. This work introduces a novel two-compartment OEMS cell for Na-ion systems, using a Na-β′′-alumina solid electrolyte (BASE) separator, whose resistance is sufficiently low to enable measurements at battery-relevant current densities. This enables the measurement of the gas evolution from an individual electrode by preventing the exchange of gases between the anode and cathode compartment…

title: An analytical model to describe self-discharge rates in solid-state batteries
abstract: Published online: 01 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41560-026-02047-0 Internal self-discharge can compromise the shelf life of solid-state batteries. Now, physico-chemical analysis of charge loss shows that the internal self-discharge over time is not solely determined by the electronic conductivity of the solid separator but also by its electrochemical stability. This model could help guide separator and cell design.

abstract: Published online: 01 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-72472-y Tracing the origins of lithium dendrite growth remains a critical challenge for lithium metal batteries. Here, the authors introduce an interfacial excess charge distribution factor to understand lithium dendrite growth, guiding the design of an electrolyte that suppresses Li dendrites in practical pouch cells.

abstract: Published online: 24 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s43246-026-01164-3 Lithium lanthanum titanium oxide is a stable, high-conductivity solid electrolyte for solid-state batteries, but the roles of lattice dynamics and local structure in lithium transport remain unclear. abstract: To address the limited accuracy and robustness of lithium-ion battery state of charge (SOC) estimation in electric vehicle battery management systems under highly dynamic conditions, this paper proposes a joint framework that combines improved online parameter identification with an adaptive square root central difference Kalman filter. The model parameters are identified online using a variable forgetting factor recursive least squares scheme driven by the predicted residual. By accumulating multi-order voltage residuals through a sliding window and adaptively adjusting the forgetting factor …

abstract: In this work, a highly efficient non-enzymatic electrochemical glucose sensor was developed using a Nickel/graphene/carbon (Ni/Gr/C) nano-electrocatalyst fabricated on a glassy carbon electrode (GCE). abstract: Sodium (Na) metal is a promising earth-abundant alternative to lithium for electrochemical energy storage; however, dendrite formation during charging of Na metal batteries remains a major hurdle. The solid electrolyte interphase (SEI), which forms on the Na metal surface, significantly influences the onset of Na dendrites. Here, we present optical microscopy observations of Na dendrite formation as a function of electrode preparation, applied current density, and choice of the organic electrolyte (diglyme vs carbonate). title: Quantifying the self-discharge rate of solid-state batteries
authors: Christoph D. Alt
Jürgen Janek
abstract: Published online: 22 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41560-026-02038-1 Internal self-discharge limits solid-state batteries because chemical potential differences drive ions and electrons to move across the separator, causing charge loss during storage. Here Janek and Alt present an analytical model that shows how tiny electronic leaks in the solid separator can cause reversible self-discharge, providing guidance for separator and cell design.

title: A Salt-Templated Ultrathin Porous Quasi-Solid-State Electrolyte Enabling High-Voltage Lithium Metal Batteries
authors: Yvhan Shi, Jingjing Ouyang, Yan Su and Guiling Wang
abstract: Quasi-solid-state electrolytes (QSSEs) are promising for safe lithium metal batteries but limited by low conductivity and high interfacial resistance. This work proposes a lithium salt pore-forming strategy to engineer efficient ion transport channels. A porous composite electrolyte (PCE) was fabricated via solution casting and selective salt removal, using lithium bis(trifluoromethanesulfonyl)imide (LiTFSI) as a dual-functional lithium source and pore-former, poly(vinylidene fluoride-hexafluoropropylene) (PVDF-HFP) as the matrix, and garnet-type Li6.4La3Zr1.4Ta0.6O12 (LLZTO) as the inorganic …

authors: Michael A. Dato, Hasnain Hafiz, Ziqi Liu, Chengi Hung, Jeffery Lopez, Juchen Guo, Khalil Amine, Meinan He and Chi-Cheung Su
abstract: Enabling high‑voltage batteries depends critically on rational electrolyte design. Conventional carbonate electrolytes with LiPF6 suffer from insufficient oxidative stability, motivating the use of lithium bis(fluorosulfonyl)imide (LiFSI) for its superior high‑temperature and high‑voltage performance. However, LiFSI is relatively costly, and its electrolytes often require high concentrations to suppress aluminum current collector corrosion in cathode architectures and to adequately passivate electrode interfaces. Here, a low‑concentration LiFSI electrolyte is realized by combining a high‑volta…

title: Improving Conductivity in Na2Ti3O7 for Na-Ion Anodes through Ti Reduction
authors: Yining Li, Pooja Kumari and Rebecca Boston
abstract: Na2Ti3O7 is a promising anode material for use in sodium ion batteries, however it suffers from low total conductivity, which is critical for conversion anode performance. Here we employ a reduction strategy, removing oxygen from the crystal structure during synthesis, aiming to increase the total conductivity through reduction of Ti ions. Using impedance spectroscopy, we show that the room temperature conductivity can be increased from 9.7 × 10–8 S.cm−1 to 4.0 × 10–7 S.cm−1 in the reduced NTO. Using cyclic voltammetry, we show that the reduced material shows a modest increase in specific capa…

title: Iron-Cation–Integrated Polyaniline Electrodes for High-Performance Coin-Cell Hybrid Supercapacitors
authors: Abdulmajid A. Mirghni, Abubakar Dahiru Shuaibu, Fatima Omar AL-Qwairi, Syed Shaheen Shah, Abdul-Rahman Al-Betar, M. Nasiruzzaman Shaikh and Md. Abdul Aziz
abstract: Polyaniline (PANI) is commonly used as an electrode material for supercapacitors; nevertheless, it has limited conductivity, leading to significant IR drops during charging and discharging. To enhance its performance, PANI was integrated with iron (Fe), a non-toxic and abundant element, creating a composite electrode. The electrochemical performance of the PANI/Fe composite, produced via direct electrodeposition, was compared to pure PANI in a 3-electrode system with 1 M H₂SO₄ electrolyte at 0.7 V vs Ag/AgCl. The PANI/Fe composite exhibited significantly reduced IR drops, suggesting improved c…

title: Early Prediction of Lithium-Ion Battery Lifetime with Random Charge-Discharge Behaviors and Feature Noise
authors: Guisong Wang, Jie Liu, Cong Wang and Yunxia Chen
abstract: The accuracy of lithium-ion battery lifetime prediction heavily relies on high-quality data, which typically exhibits complete charge-discharge cycles and low feature noise. However, accurate lifetime prediction faces significant challenges when batteries are subject to random charge-discharge behaviors and intrinsic feature noise. To address this issue, this paper proposes an early prediction framework suitable for such conditions. The proposed approach incorporates an adaptive variable window moving average (AVWMA) filter, which utilizes change-point analysis (CPA), along with a dynamic wind…

abstract: Zero-excess Li metal batteries (ZELMB), also called “anode-free” LMBs, promise high energy, low cost, and reduced complexity by abandoning traditional negative electrode materials. title: Fast & Reliable End-of-Life Evaluation of Li-Ion Batteries in Electric Vehicles: A Dual Metric Approach for Ageing Heterogeneity Assessment
authors: Adrien Najjar-Giudicelli, Sylvain Lespinats, Nicolas Guillet and Pierre-Xavier Thivel
abstract: As electric vehicles (EVs) are expected to gradually replace internal combustion engine vehicles, managing end-of-life (EoL) batteries is crucial. This work proposes a two-step framework for state-of-health (SoH) diagnosis of retired EV lithium-ion batteries, determining whether they should be recycled or refurbished for second-life applications. The framework uses an impedance-based sorting algorithm along with a purposeful use of an incremental capacity (IC)-derived method. A standardised protocol was applied to 46 battery elements from two retired Nissan LEAF Gen. 1 packs. Unexpected result…

title: State of Energy Estimation of a Lithium-Ion Battery Based on Different Regression Techniques
authors: Shivanshu Kumar, Marla Sahithi Kalpavalli and Amalendu Bikash Choudhury
abstract: Battery Management Systems (BMS) are rapidly evolving due to the rising utilization of lithium-ion batteries (LiBs), especially in electric vehicles (EVs). One of the crucial metrics in BMS is the State of Energy (SOE) which plays a vital role in assessing battery performance and ensuring effective energy management. This study examines the application of four machine learning regression methods namely Random Forest (RF), Support Vector Regression (SVR), Decision Trees (DT), and Gradient Boosting (GB) for a precise estimate of SOE. The evaluation employs empirical data obtained from the Brauns…
